diff:
diff --git a/src/Carbon/Traits/Difference.php b/src/Carbon/Traits/Difference.php
index db5fe1d..370976a 100644
--- a/src/Carbon/Traits/Difference.php
+++ b/src/Carbon/Traits/Difference.php
@@ -468,6 +468,13 @@ trait Difference
      * echo Carbon::tomorrow()->diffForHumans(['parts' => 2]) . "\n";
      * echo Carbon::tomorrow()->diffForHumans(['parts' => 3, 'join' => true]) . "\n";
      * echo Carbon::tomorrow()->diffForHumans(Carbon::yesterday()) . "\n";
      * echo Carbon::tomorrow()->diffForHumans(Carbon::yesterday(), ['short' => true]) . "\n";
+     * // Sub-second precision using 'minimumUnit':
+     * $a = Carbon::parse('2024-01-01 00:00:00.000000');
+     * $b = Carbon::parse('2024-01-01 00:00:00.500000');
+     * echo $b->diffForHumans($a, ['minimumUnit' => 'millisecond']) . "\n"; // "500 milliseconds after"
+     * echo $b->diffForHumans($a, ['minimumUnit' => 'microsecond']) . "\n"; // "500000 microseconds after"
+     * $c = Carbon::parse('2024-01-01 00:00:00.000250');
+     * echo $c->diffForHumans($a, ['minimumUnit' => 'microsecond']) . "\n"; // "250 microseconds after"
      * ```
      *
      * @param Carbon|DateTimeInterface|string|array|null $other
@@ -494,7 +501,10 @@ trait Difference
      *                                                            `  - if $join is missing, a space will be used as glue
      *                                                            ⦿ 'other' entry (see above)
      *                                                            ⦿ 'minimumUnit' entry determines the smallest unit of time to display can be long or
-     *                                                            `  short form of the units, e.g. 'hour' or 'h' (default value: s)
+     *                                                            `  short form of the units, e.g. 'hour' or 'h' (default value: s).
+     *                                                            `  Use 'millisecond'/'ms' or 'microsecond'/'µs' to enable sub-second
+     *                                                            `  precision — without this, diffs smaller than 1 second always show
+     *                                                            `  "0 seconds".
      *                                                            ⦿ 'locale' language in which the diff should be output (has no effect if 'translator' key is set)
      *                                                            ⦿ 'translator' a custom translator to use to translator the output.

excluded_defs.name: NO_ZERO_DIFF
excluded_defs.reason: opened to check if a suitable bitmask constant already existed; not needed because minimumUnit array key is the right mechanism

exploration_log.aha_moment: The $diffIntervalArray in forHumans() already includes millisecond and microsecond rows; the loop only breaks early at the minimumUnit — setting minimumUnit to 'microsecond' makes the loop traverse past seconds and pick up sub-second values.
exploration_log.hindsight: Check the existing docblock for minimumUnit before assuming the feature is missing; it was already listed but without the explanation of why it matters for sub-second precision.

alternatives: Add MICROSECOND_DIFF bitmask constant to DiffOptions.php
key_decisions.decision: Update docblock rather than add a new constant/bitmask
key_decisions.reasoning: The minimumUnit option already works end-to-end; adding a redundant constant would duplicate semantics without benefit. Documenting the existing feature is the minimal correct fix.

search_sequence.result: diffForHumans() delegates to $this->diff($other)->forHumans($syntax,...) where $syntax can be an array with minimumUnit

search_sequence.reasoning: Needed to confirm whether microsecond precision was already supported
search_sequence.result: minimumUnit defaults to 's'; the diffIntervalArray includes millisecond and microsecond entries; loop breaks when minimumUnit matches current unit

search_sequence.action: Traced loop logic for a 500µs diff with minimumUnit='microsecond'
search_sequence.reasoning: Verified the existing mechanism already works; only documentation was missing
search_sequence.result: The loop skips all zero-value entries including seconds and correctly adds the microsecond entry, producing '500 microseconds'

solve_notes: The underlying `minimumUnit` option in `forHumans()` already supports 'microsecond' and 'millisecond' — when set, the iteration loop in `CarbonInterval::forHumans()` doesn't break at the 'second' entry and instead continues to sub-second entries in `$diffIntervalArray`. The fix documents this capability explicitly in `diffForHumans()` with code examples and a clarifying note explaining why the default 's' masks sub-second diffs.
task_text:
Fix diffForHumans not handling micro-second precision for sub-second diffs

The `diffForHumans()` method in `Traits/Difference.php` reports diffs
down to seconds but does not include microsecond-level precision. When
two Carbon instances differ by less than one second, the output is
"0 seconds ago" rather than a microsecond-accurate representation. Add
a `microsecond` resolution option so `diffForHumans()` can report
"500 milliseconds ago" or "250 microseconds ago" when configured.

thrash_preventing_defs.name: getForHumansParameters
thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: shows that $minimumUnit defaults to 's' and is overridden by extract($this->getForHumansInitialVariables($syntax, $short)) — agent would search here to confirm array options flow through

thrash_preventing_defs.name: getForHumansInitialVariables
thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: confirms that when $syntax is an array, all its keys (including minimumUnit) are returned verbatim and extracted as local variables, preventing confusion about whether array options are lost

tier_difference_reasoning: The minimum set (diffForHumans + forHumans) is enough for a human who can trace the array extraction chain from reading the code. The thrash_preventing set adds getForHumansParameters and getForHumansInitialVariables because an AI agent would search for 'how does minimumUnit propagate from diffForHumans to forHumans?' — without seeing these methods, it might wrongly conclude the option is silently discarded or that a new code path is needed.
